summary: Historic Half-Step→Franklin's debut, rare Werewolves encore
blurb: The set opens with the historic Mississippi Half‑Step into Franklin’s Tower and builds through a powerful second set highlighted by a sprawling Dancin’ jam and an expansive drum/space segment. Standout moments include a blazing Lazy Lightning→Supplication pair and a rare Werewolves Of London encore.
review: May 17, 1978 at Chicago’s Uptown Theater captures a pivotal moment in Dead history – the first ever pairing of Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo straight into Franklin’s Tower. Jerry’s lead work shines on that transition, delivering crisp phrasing and a solid slide while Keith adds his signature ‘drippy’ 1978 keyboard textures. The second set rolls forward with a marathon Dancin’ In The Street jam that blossoms into a ~15‑minute drum excursion before launching into Terrapin Station, Not Fade Away and Around & Around. Highlights abound: Lazy Lightning segues flawlessly into Supplication, Bob Weir doubles down on Me & My Uncle → Big River with fiery interplay, and the night closes on a rare Werewolves Of London encore that fans still talk about.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the historic Half‑Step→Franklin’s transition, the rare Werewolves encore, and strong performances across the set. Multiple sources highlight the same standout songs (Half‑Step/Franklin’s, Lazy Lightning→Supplication, Terrapin Station) and note tight playing from the entire band, indicating a consistently enthusiastic consensus.

best_recording.reason: Stunning Seamons matrix capture – excellent, well‑balanced sound that showcases the historic Half‑Step→Franklin’s transition and overall band dynamics without noticeable flaws.

key_highlights: First ever Half‑Step → Franklin’s Tower transition
Extended ~15‑minute drum/space segment feeding into Terrapin Station

band_performance.Jerry: Delivered strong lead work throughout, highlighted by a crisp solo on Werewolves and tight phrasing on the historic Half‑Step→Franklin’s transition; vocal strain on Franklin’s verses but overall solid.
band_performance.Phil: Kept a reliable low end, his bass stayed steady despite an odd string break mid‑show and even added an excited scream during the space jam.
band_performance.Bob: In fine form, trading fiery leads on Me & My Uncle → Big River and adding a memorable slide during the Werewolves encore.
band_performance.Keith: Provided his characteristic ‘drippy’ 1978 keyboard textures with tasteful fills on Tennessee Jed and a bright solo after the Not Fade Away intro.
band_performance.Mickey: Drove an expansive ~15‑minute drum excursion that propelled the Space segment, punctuating transitions with punchy bass‑drum accents, especially during the Not Fade Away intro.
band_performance.Billy: Partnered with Mickey in the marathon drum jam, delivering powerful rhythmic power and seamless flow into the second‑set climax.
